flashcards.front: What is the purpose of sigpending() function?
flashcards.back: The sigpending() function examines pending signals - signals that have been generated but are currently blocked (held pending) because of the process's signal mask.

flashcards.front: What header file is required for sigpending()?
flashcards.back: #include <signal.h> is required for sigpending() function and related signal handling structures.

flashcards.front: What is the function signature of sigpending()?
flashcards.back: int sigpending(sigset_t *set); - It takes a pointer to a sigset_t object and returns 0 on success, -1 on error.
A pending signal is a signal that has been generated but has not yet been delivered because it is currently blocked by the process's signal mask.

flashcards.front: What is sigset_t in signal handling?
flashcards.back: sigset_t is a data type used to represent a set of signals. It is a bitmap or array that tracks which signals are in the set.

flashcards.front: How does sigpending() differ from sigprocmask()?
flashcards.back: sigpending() is used to examine pending (blocked) signals, while sigprocmask() is used to change the signal mask (block/unblock signals).
The signal mask is the set of signals that are currently blocked from delivery to a process. It determines which signals will be held pending.

flashcards.front: What function initializes an empty signal set?
flashcards.back: int sigemptyset(sigset_t *set); - It initializes set to empty (no signals) and returns 0 on success.

flashcards.front: What function adds a signal to a signal set?
flashcards.back: int sigaddset(sigset_t *set, int signum); - It adds signal 'signum' to 'set' and returns 0 on success.

flashcards.front: What function checks if a signal is in a set?
flashcards.back: int sigismember(const sigset_t *set, int signum); - Returns 1 if signum is in set, 0 if not, -1 on error.

flashcards.front: What happens when a blocked signal is generated?
flashcards.back: When a blocked signal is generated, it becomes pending. The OS maintains a pending signal set for the process. When the signal is unblocked, it will be delivered.

flashcards.front: Can multiple instances of the same signal be pending?
flashcards.back: No, at most one instance of each signal type can be pending at a time. Multiple occurrences of the same signal are merged into one.
Signal delivery is the process where the kernel transfers a pending signal to the process, invoking the signal handler or taking the default action.

flashcards.front: What is the relationship between sigpending() and real-time signals?
flashcards.back: For real-time signals (SIGRTMIN to SIGRTMAX), multiple pending signals are queued. sigpending() shows which signal types are pending.
int sigfillset(sigset_t *set); - It initializes set to contain all signals and returns 0 on success.
